name: SaaS Account Health Analysis
description: Explores SaaS account data: ARR by plan tier, churn risk segmentation, seat utilization gaps, and expansion opportunity scoring.

narration.pre: Starting a SaaS account health analysis. We'll explore the customer base to understand revenue concentration, churn risk, utilization, and expansion potential.
Importing SaaS account data: 10,000 accounts with plan tier, industry, ARR, seat utilization, churn risk, NPS score, support tickets, and expansion opportunity flags.
narration.post: Dataset loaded: 10,000 accounts, 20 columns. Key metrics: annual_recurring_revenue, seat_utilization, churn_risk (High/Medium/Low), feature_adoption_rate, and expansion_opportunity flag.

narration.pre: Inspect the schema to see the column types. SaaS data mixes financial metrics (ARR), usage metrics (seats, adoption), and risk categories.
narration.post: 20 columns. annual_recurring_revenue is the monetary field — it will display as formatted currency. plan_tier, industry, and churn_risk are categorical segmentation columns. seat_utilization and feature_adoption_rate are percentages.

narration.pre: AI generates automatic insights from the column structure.
narration.post: Insights ready — ARR concentration by plan tier, churn risk distribution, and seat utilization patterns identified. Each insight card has Explore in Query.

details.sql: SELECT plan_tier, COUNT(*) AS accounts, ROUND(SUM(annual_recurring_revenue), 2) AS total_arr, ROUND(AVG(annual_recurring_revenue), 2) AS avg_arr, ROUND(AVG(seat_utilization), 2) AS avg_utilization FROM dataset GROUP BY plan_tier ORDER BY total_arr DESC

narration.pre: ARR by plan tier: how is revenue distributed across Free, Starter, Professional, and Enterprise tiers? This reveals revenue concentration and tier adoption patterns.
narration.post: 4 plan tiers compared. Enterprise accounts typically drive the most ARR with highest average revenue per account. Utilization rates by tier show whether customers are getting value — low utilization is a leading indicator of churn.

details.sql: SELECT churn_risk, COUNT(*) AS accounts, ROUND(SUM(annual_recurring_revenue), 2) AS at_risk_arr, ROUND(AVG(nps), 1) AS avg_nps, ROUND(AVG(support_tickets_90d), 1) AS avg_tickets FROM dataset GROUP BY churn_risk ORDER BY at_risk_arr DESC

narration.pre: Churn risk segmentation: how much ARR is at risk by churn category? And what do high-risk accounts look like in terms of NPS and support ticket volume?
narration.post: 3 risk segments. The critical number is at-risk ARR in the High churn bucket — that's the revenue the customer success team needs to protect this quarter. High-risk accounts with low NPS and high ticket volume are the most urgent cases.

details.sql: SELECT industry, COUNT(*) AS accounts, ROUND(SUM(annual_recurring_revenue), 2) AS total_arr, ROUND(AVG(feature_adoption_rate), 2) AS avg_adoption FROM dataset GROUP BY industry ORDER BY total_arr DESC LIMIT 10

narration.pre: Industry breakdown: which verticals contribute the most ARR, and which have the highest feature adoption? Industries with high ARR but low adoption may need vertical-specific enablement.
narration.post: 6 industries ranked. Feature adoption rate by industry reveals product-market fit differences — high adoption industries are natural expansion targets, while low adoption industries may need different onboarding approaches.

details.sql: SELECT account_id, plan_tier, industry, annual_recurring_revenue, seat_utilization, churn_risk, nps FROM dataset WHERE seat_utilization < 0.3 ORDER BY annual_recurring_revenue DESC LIMIT 20

narration.pre: Under-utilized accounts: accounts using less than 30% of their licensed seats. These are at high risk of downsizing or churning — but they're also candidates for re-engagement if the product isn't meeting their needs.
narration.post: Top 20 under-utilized accounts by ARR. These are the highest-value accounts at risk of seat reduction. A customer success team would prioritize these for proactive outreach — understand why utilization is low and drive adoption before renewal.

details.sql: SELECT plan_tier, expansion_opportunity, COUNT(*) AS accounts, ROUND(SUM(annual_recurring_revenue), 2) AS current_arr, ROUND(AVG(seat_utilization), 2) AS avg_util FROM dataset WHERE expansion_opportunity = 'Y' GROUP BY plan_tier, expansion_opportunity ORDER BY current_arr DESC

narration.pre: Expansion pipeline: accounts flagged for expansion opportunity, broken out by current plan tier. High-utilization accounts on lower tiers are natural upsell candidates.
narration.post: 4 tiers with expansion-flagged accounts. The biggest upsell opportunity is typically Professional-to-Enterprise upgrades on accounts with high utilization — they're already using the product heavily and could benefit from Enterprise features.

Session summary: Analyzed 10,000 SaaS accounts across 4 plan tiers and 6 industries. Quantified at-risk ARR by churn segment, identified under-utilized high-value accounts, and sized the expansion pipeline by tier.

resume_state.last_sql: SELECT plan_tier, expansion_opportunity, COUNT(*) AS accounts, ROUND(SUM(annual_recurring_revenue), 2) AS current_arr, ROUND(AVG(seat_utilization), 2) AS avg_util FROM dataset WHERE expansion_opportunity = 'Y' GROUP BY plan_tier, expansion_opportunity ORDER BY current_arr DESC
